diagnosis: The washer is not draining and stops before completing the normal cycle due to a drain pump problem.
recommended_steps: Unplug the washer from the electrical outlet for safety
Locate the drain pump, typically found at the bottom front of the washer behind the access panel
Remove any visible debris, lint, or foreign objects blocking the pump inlet
Check the drain hose for kinks or blockages and straighten if needed
Inspect the pump impeller for damage and replace if cracked or broken
Reassemble all components and run a test cycle with no load to verify drainage
If the pump is damaged beyond cleaning, order a replacement drain pump assembly (typically $50-150) and follow manufacturer installation instructions
